extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won bunch sprint after POGAČAR took 6 bonus seconds at late intermediate sprint following failed attack phase, positioning best among GC contenders.
tactical_patterns: Pogačar hunting intermediate sprint bonuses with 5.9km remaining, forcing Vingegaard to chase without gaining seconds reveals 2023 micro-gains emphasis.
Quick-Step allowing UAE/Jumbo attacks to expire 16-20km out rather than chasing immediately, preserving sprint train energy for finale.
Neilson Powless attacking penultimate 12% climb after crash fragmented peloton shows EF targeting transition chaos over pure climbing.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: aggressively targeting bonus seconds at intermediate sprints on rolling stages rather than waiting for mountains, adapting tactics for marginal gains
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: made split when Pogačar attacked but lost intermediate sprint seconds, suggesting less focus on stage racing minutiae than rival
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: attacking twice on short punchy climbs 20km and 16km from finish shows confidence after early-season stage race win
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: Quick-Step sprint execution remains reference-level despite late GC contender attacks fragmenting positioning
rider_signals.GALNØYCK Thymen: negative: crashed during crucial transition phase before attacks, UAE losing domestique when needed most
